channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Fueled by boos, Browns receiver Jerry Jeudy turns in 235-yard performance in return to Denver
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The boos only brought out the best in Jerry Jeudy as he returned to Denver. "They only boo you,” Jeudy reasoned, “when they know there’s something great in you.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Broncos outlast Jameis Winston's 497 passing yards to beat Browns in primetime
item.description: The Denver Broncos outlasted the Cleveland Browns and Jameis Winston's 497 passing yards to pick up a big win for playoff implications on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mollie Marcoux Samaan stepping down as LPGA commissioner after 3 1/2 years of record prize money
item.description: Mollie Marcoux Samaan is resigning in January as LPGA commissioner, a surprise announcement Monday after record growth in prize money along with criticism the LPGA wasn't gaining in popularity during the surge in women's sports. Marcoux Samaan is leaving on Jan.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: USC QB Miller Moss enters transfer portal after losing starting job to Jayden Maiava
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Southern California quarterback Miller Moss is entering the transfer portal after losing the Trojans' starting job last month. Moss made his announcement on social media Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida State hires Nebraska's Tony White as defensive coordinator, AP source says
item.description: Florida State hired Tony White to be its defensive coordinator Monday, poaching him from Nebraska after the Cornhuskers posted top-20 defenses in consecutive years, a person familiar with the decision told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: McCaffrey injury potentially rules him out for rest of season
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — San Francisco running back Christian McCaffrey suffered a knee injury that coach Kyle Shanahan described as “potentially” season-ending in the 49ers’ 35-10 loss to the Buffalo Bills on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Golden State Valkyries unveil season games, home opener
item.description: (KRON) – The newest Bay Area major pro sports team, the Golden State Valkyries, has announced the date of its inaugural season at the Chase Center on Monday. The inaugural game, slated for May 16 of next year, will be a California battle with the Valkyries facing the Los Angeles Sparks.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Ryan Poles to remain Bears general manager and lead search for new head coach
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. (AP) — Ryan Poles will remain the Chicago Bears' general manager and serve as the “point person” in the search for a head coach to replace the fired Matt Eberflus, team president Kevin Warren said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 49ers' Christian McCaffrey suffered 'potentially' season-ending knee injury vs Bills, coach says
item.description: San Francisco 49ers star Christian McCaffrey could "potentially" be lost for the season with a knee injury, head coach Kyle Shanahan said Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cubs add Matthew Boyd to rotation with $29 million, 2-year contract, AP source says
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Chicago Cubs have added Matthew Boyd to their rotation in their first big offseason move, agreeing to a $29 million, two-year contract with the veteran left-hander, according to a person familiar with the negotiations.
item.dc:creator: JAY COHEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Digital Sports' college football winners and losers: Week 14
item.description: There were a handful of upsets during the college football weekend. But no one will be talking about that as flag fights marred the interesting slate of matchups.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Christian McCaffrey's season 'potentially' over after knee injury in 49ers' loss to Bills
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — San Francisco running back Christian McCaffrey suffered a knee injury that coach Kyle Shanahan described as “potentially” season-ending in the 49ers’ 35-10 loss to the Buffalo Bills on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: McLaughlin's field goal in OT helps Bucs beat Panthers 26-23, move into 1st-place tie in NFC South
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers beat the Carolina Panthers 26-23 in overtime on a 30-yard field goal by Chase McLaughlin on Sunday to move into a tie for first place in the NFC South.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utes close disappointing season with 28-14 victory
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (ABC4 Sports) - Utah forced three turnovers and scored two defensive touchdowns Friday night, including a 60-yard pick-6 by Zemaiah Vaughn, as the Utes snapped a seven-game losing streak with a 28-14 victory over UCF.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Spurgeon breaks 33-game scoreless streak with two goals, Minnesota beats Chicago 3-2
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Jared Spurgeon ended a 33-game scoring drought with two of Minnesota’s three second-period goals and the Wild beat the Chicago Blackhawks 3-2 on Friday. Marco Rossi also scored for Minnesota and Marc-Andre Fleury, who turned 40 on Thursday, made 20 saves to improve to 5-0-1.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Blake Snell and Dodgers agree to $182 million, 5-year contract, AP source says
item.description: Less than a month after winning the World Series, the Los Angeles Dodgers are spending big again to add one of baseball's best pitchers to their star-studded roster.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Wembanyama scores 34 as Spurs beat Jazz, 128-115
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - 17 games into the NBA season, the Utah Jazz and San Antonio Spurs have already played each other four times, and the Jazz have to be glad they don't have to see Victor Wembanyama again this season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
